session.title: Designing Mountain Warfare Course Assessments and Branching Scenarios
session.topic_summary: The session develops a Small Unit Leadership in Mountain Warfare (SULMW) course in Moodle, including quiz configuration for five modules and a final exam, competency framework design, and competency-linked learning plans. It also designs and iteratively refines a complex H5P branching scenario and authoring checklist for a patrol-based tactical decision game aligned to mountain warfare learning objectives SULMW4-1 through SULMW4-4. The conversation includes troubleshooting of scoring, routing logic, and after-action review behavior to ensure pedagogically sound performance assessment.

competencies.name: Data-Driven Improvement and QA of Learning Objects

axes.label: Design quality
axes.description: Depth and rigor of course, assessment, and scenario design across quizzes, competencies, and H5P structures.

axes.label: Technical fluency
axes.description: Effective use and configuration of Moodle quizzes, competency frameworks, and H5P branching scenarios to meet learning goals.

axes.label: Strategic alignment
axes.description: Alignment of activities with SULMW learning objectives, competency frameworks, and leadership outcomes.

axes.label: Learning analytics
axes.description: Use of scoring schemes, completion rules, and after-action reviews to generate actionable performance data.

axes.label: Reflective practice
axes.description: Identification and correction of design flaws, including routing and scoring issues, to improve learner experience and validity.

blooms_progression.label: Remember
blooms_progression.title: Recall of Structures and Settings
blooms_progression.description: The session repeatedly references specific quiz settings (e.g., 5 random questions, 100-point totals, passing grade 80) and named learning objectives SULMW1-1 through SULMW5-4, indicating accurate recall of configuration details and objective labels.

blooms_progression.label: Understand
blooms_progression.title: Conceptual Understanding of Competencies
blooms_progression.description: The design work shows understanding of how learning objectives, competencies, and activities relate, such as using learning objective text as competency descriptions and ensuring competencies are awarded upon course completion.

blooms_progression.label: Apply
blooms_progression.title: Applying Principles in Tools
blooms_progression.description: Principles of assessment and competency-based education are applied in concrete LMS actions, like configuring random-question quizzes, linking competencies to course completion, and turning doctrinal objectives into H5P decision nodes.

blooms_progression.label: Analyze
blooms_progression.title: Analyzing Flow and Failure Modes
blooms_progression.description: The session dissects branching logic problems (e.g., early termination, skipped phases, looping endings), analyzes why certain options bypass decision points, and proposes or validates structural fixes for accurate scoring and learner flow.

blooms_progression.label: Evaluate
blooms_progression.title: Evaluating Design Tradeoffs
blooms_progression.description: There is ongoing evaluation of tradeoffs between aggressive versus cautious tactical routes, comfort versus survivability in defensive positions, and different resupply strategies, framed in terms of mission risk, learning impact, and scoring consequences.

blooms_progression.label: Create
blooms_progression.title: Creating Integrated Learning Assets
blooms_progression.description: The session creates a new integrated learning ecosystem: a SULMW competency framework, quiz suite, and a multi-phase H5P branching scenario with authored decisions, scoring, endings, and a NotebookLM video prompt focused on the optimal leadership path.

timeline.title: Module Quiz Architecture Established
timeline.description: Defined and implemented quiz settings for Modules 1–5 and the final exam, including random-question banks, 100-point scoring, pass thresholds, unlimited attempts where appropriate, and completion conditions.

timeline.title: Competency Framework and Naming Convention Finalized
timeline.description: Created the Small Unit Leadership in Mountain Warfare competency framework with SULMW1-1 through SULMW5-4, applying the naming convention to learning objective pages and using objective text as competency descriptions.

timeline.title: Competencies Linked to Course and Learning Plans
timeline.description: Added all SULMW competencies to the course, configured them to be completed upon course completion, and integrated them with a learning plan template for structured tracking.

timeline.title: Tactical Decision Game Converted to Scenario Design
timeline.description: Transformed a textual mountain warfare tactical decision game into a storyboard for a self-paced H5P branching scenario aligned to objectives SULMW4-1 to SULMW4-4.

timeline.title: H5P Branching Scenario Authoring Guide Produced
timeline.description: Developed a detailed authoring guide and checklist for the 'Patrol to Saddle 2140' scenario, specifying phases, decision points, scoring allocations, feedback texts, and media suggestions.

timeline.title: Branching Logic and Scoring Debugged
timeline.description: Identified issues where early decisions skipped intermediate phases or ended the scenario prematurely, then re-routed options so all paths progress through all decision points and accumulate scores for a meaningful after-action review.

timeline.title: Optimal Path and Video Prompt Defined
timeline.description: Articulated the optimal leadership decision path in the branching scenario and authored a NotebookLM video prompt emphasizing good decisions in planning, logistics, weapons handling, and defensive positioning.

employer_value.title: End-to-End Learning Systems Designer
employer_value.description: Demonstrated the ability to architect a complete competency-based course—quizzes, frameworks, and completion logic—using an LMS and content tools to align learning activities with operational objectives and measurable outcomes.

employer_value.title: Scenario-Based Training and Simulation Builder
employer_value.description: Designed and refined an H5P branching scenario that translates complex mountain warfare doctrine into realistic, scored decision-making exercises with rich feedback and multiple endings for leadership development.

employer_value.title: Learning Quality and Reliability Engineer
employer_value.description: Diagnosed and corrected logic and scoring defects in interactive content, ensuring every learner path yields valid scores, comprehensive after-action reviews, and accurate reflection of performance across all decision points.

employer_value.title: Domain-Aware Instructional Technologist
employer_value.description: Integrated USMC mountain warfare doctrine and safety principles into assessments and scenarios, preserving tactical authenticity while optimizing pedagogy and learner engagement.

portfolio.title: Small Unit Leadership in Mountain Warfare – AI-Supported Course Build
portfolio.subtitle: SELF-DIRECTED · APPLIED · DOCUMENTED

documentation_formats.label: LMS Configuration Screenshots and Settings Export
After-Action Report Samples and Score Logs
Short Explainer Video of Optimal Leadership Path

meta.notes: Scoring reflects strong performance in instructional design, competency alignment, and scenario authoring evidenced across multiple detailed logs, but is constrained to what is observable in the session (not overall mastery). ROI estimates assume typical market rates for bespoke scenario-based e-learning and SME-supported course builds in a defense context.
